title: lavandula stoechas subsp. maderensis
intro: Lavandula stoechas subsp. maderensis, also known as Madeira Dyer's lavender, is a subspecies of the Dyer's lavender plant, native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Lavandula stoechas subsp. maderensis include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, essential oil, and tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
